open_issues.title: Memory leak in process monitor loop
open_issues.body: After running for ~30 minutes the RSS climbs steadily. Suspect the BTreeMap inside the polling thread isn't being pruned. Happens on Linux only so far.

open_issues.title: Add async support for disk I/O reads
open_issues.body: The disk stat reads are still blocking. We should move them to tokio::fs so the main event loop doesn't stall under heavy load.

open_issues.title: CPU usage spikes when > 64 processes tracked
open_issues.body: Profiled with flamegraph — the sort step inside collect_metrics() is O(n log n) every tick. Needs caching or a smarter data structure.

open_issues.title: Config file not reloaded on SIGHUP
open_issues.body: Expected behavior: send SIGHUP to the daemon and it re-reads tracker.toml without restart. Currently the signal is caught but reload() is never called.

open_issues.title: Document tracker.toml schema
open_issues.body: New contributors keep misconfiguring the polling_interval and threshold fields. We need either inline comments in the example config or a proper schema doc.

open_issues.title: Windows build fails: missing libc dependency
open_issues.body: Cross-compiling for Windows errors on the libc crate. Might need to gate the unix-specific code behind #[cfg(unix)].

open_issues.title: Expose metrics via HTTP endpoint
open_issues.body: Would be useful to have a /metrics route (Prometheus-compatible) so this can plug into existing monitoring stacks without extra tooling.

open_issues.title: Panic on startup if tracker.toml is missing
open_issues.body: Should degrade gracefully with a helpful error message rather than unwrap() panicking. Easy fix but affects first-time users badly.
